name: Orellana la Vieja and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer Loop
name_original: Subida al Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer desde Orellana La Vieja — circular por la Zona de Interés Regional Embalse de Orellana y Sierra de Pela
description: This difficult 74.8-mile road cycling route climbs to the Castle of Puebla de Alcocer, offering panoramic views over the Embalse de Orellana
region_intro: Road cycling around Orellana La Vieja offers diverse landscapes in the heart of Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by the expansive Orellana Reservoir, often referred to as the "Sweet Coast," which provides a unique inland aquatic environment. Cyclists can explore the characteristic Extremaduran dehesa, featuring oak trees and green pastures, alongside routes that follow the Guadiana River. The terrain varies from mostly well-paved surfaces to routes with significant elevation gains, catering to different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Orellana La Vieja The most popular road cycling route is Orellana la Vieja and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer Loop, a 74.8 miles (120.4 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 43 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive views around the Orellana Reservoir and includes significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Don Benito – La Haba loop from Don Benito, a moderate 51.6 miles (83.1 km) path. This route traverses the local countryside, connecting towns with a mix of rolling hills and flatter sections. Local road cyclists also love the Vineyard View – Orellana Dam loop from Valdivia, a 29.1 miles (46.8 km) trail leading through vineyard landscapes and past the Orellana Dam, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Orellana La Vieja is defined by the vast Orellana Reservoir, the scenic Extremaduran dehesa, and routes along the Guadiana River. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging long-distance circuits. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Orellana La Vieja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Orellana La Vieja?
A: Road cycling around Orellana La Vieja offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find mostly well-paved surfaces suitable for road bikes, with routes traversing the characteristic Extremaduran dehesa, featuring oak trees and green pastures. The area also includes routes along the Guadiana River and around the expansive Orellana Reservoir. Elevation gains vary significantly, from easier rides with up to 1,000 feet of climbing to more demanding options with over 3,000 feet of ascent.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While many routes offer significant elevation, there are options for varying fitness levels. For a moderate experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyard View – Orellana Dam loop from Valdivia&lt;/a&gt;. This 29.1-mile (46.8 km) route leads through vineyard landscapes and past the Orellana Dam, offering a more manageable ride.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the Orellana La Vieja region?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with substantial elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992992123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana la Vieja and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 74.8-mile (120.4 km) route with over 4,600 feet (1,400 meters) of elevation, providing extensive views around the Orellana Reservoir. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana de la Sierra – Villanueva de la Serena loop from Navalvillar de Pela&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 106 km with nearly 4,000 feet (1,200 meters) of climbing.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You'll cycle past the vast Orellana Reservoir, often called the 'Sweet Coast,' and through the scenic Extremaduran dehesa. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5666878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and Palace of the Orellana&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5666953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana Dam&lt;/a&gt;, and the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5666635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana de la Sierra Village&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5667110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Orellana&lt;/a&gt;, Spain's first inland Blue Flag beach, is also a significant point of interest.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes in the area are designed as loops. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992992123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana la Vieja and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer Loop&lt;/a&gt; encircles a significant part of the reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Benito – La Haba loop from Don Benito&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyard View – Orellana Dam loop from Valdivia&lt;/a&gt; are also excellent circular options that allow you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Orellana La Vieja?
A: The region of Extremadura generally experiences hot summers. For road cycling, the most pleasant times are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant, making for more comfortable and scenic rides.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Orellana La Vieja is a key point along the Guadiana Natural Path, offering long-distance cycling opportunities. Beyond the specific routes listed, options like 'Orellana a Almendralejo' span about 119.1 km, and a route from Orellana la Vieja to Navalvillar extends 83.6 km. These longer routes often feature mostly well-paved surfaces, ideal for extended road cycling tours.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Orellana La Vieja?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the vast Orellana Reservoir to the scenic Extremaduran dehesa. The variety of routes, catering to different fitness levels, is also highly appreciated. Many enjoy the well-paved surfaces and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites like the Castle and Palace of Orellana.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: While specific cafes are not detailed for every route, the towns and villages connected by these cycling paths, such as Orellana La Vieja, Don Benito, Valdivia, and Villanueva de la Serena, offer amenities where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops for refreshments and supplies. Planning your route with these towns in mind will ensure convenient stops.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly road cycling in Orellana La Vieja?
A: While many routes involve significant distances and elevation, some sections or shorter loops might be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with road cycling. The region's connection to the Guadiana Natural Path suggests flatter, more accessible sections, particularly the stage from Orellana to Medellín, which has a smoother, more rolling profile. Always check the route's distance and elevation profile to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Orellana La Vieja?
A: There are several road cycling routes available around Orellana La Vieja. Komoot features 8 distinct road cycling tours in this guide, with 6 categorized as moderate and 2 as difficult, offering a good selection for various skill levels.
tips: Orellana de la Sierra is a town located in the old middle of the Sierra de Pela, covered by its northern slope but elevated above the rest of the terrain. It is a good town to see the whole of the Orellana Reservoir.

The Serena Reservoir is one of the largest reservoirs in Badajoz and where a road crosses it, skirting a mountain called Masatrigo. This is a curious passage through the swamp but beautiful for the views it offers.

The castle of Puebla de Alcocer is an imposing castle built during the 12th and 13th centuries, remodeled in the 15th century by Don Gutierre de Sotomayor, Master of the Order of Alcántara, thanks to the granting of a license from the King of Castile, Juan II.

It is located on an elongated rocky mass, at the highest point of Puebla de Alcocer, from where the movements of the adversaries were monitored for kilometers around. Today the regions of La Serena and La Siberia are contemplated and the fortification can be seen from several points.

faqs.question: How long does this racebike tour typically take?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike tour, covering approximately 120 kilometers with over 1400 meters of elevation gain, typically takes around 5 hours and 43 minutes to complete. This duration is for cycling time only and does not include stops.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for road cycling?
faqs.answer: While the route is designed for racebikes and Orellana la Vieja is a cycle tourism hub, it's important to note that the ascent to Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer may involve an old stone road, and a section connecting Puebla de Alcocer to Orellana la Vieja is described as a 'well-marked, passable, sand track.' These sections could be challenging for typical road bicycles. The rest of the route generally follows secondary roads.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this tour in Orellana La Vieja?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Orellana La Vieja, which serves as the starting and ending point for this circular tour. Specific parking areas for cyclists are usually available within the town, often near the main square or public facilities.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features diverse terrain, including extensive oak forests (dehesas), meadows, olive groves, and a mix of river valley and plain landscapes. Be prepared for varied surfaces, especially near Puebla de Alcocer Castle, where you might encounter older stone or sand tracks.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and landscapes along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the beautiful Regional Interest Zone Embalse de Orellana y Sierra de Pela. The route skirts the Orellana Reservoir, the largest freshwater reservoir in Extremadura, and offers panoramic views of the La Serena Reservoir from the castle. The area is rich in biodiversity, recognized as a Site of Community Importance (SCI) and a Special Protection Area for Birds (SPA).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in Extremadura, including this route, is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, temperatures are milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: What are the key landmarks and viewpoints on this tour?
faqs.answer: Key landmarks include the historic 12th-13th century Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer, which offers wonderful panoramic views. You'll also pass through the settlement of Orellana de la Sierra and skirt the impressive Masatrigo Mountain and La Serena Reservoir.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails. You'll find it passes through parts of the Camino de los Mineros - Etapa 3, Camino Natural del Guadiana, and Camino del Condado de Belalcázar - Etapa 3, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for cycling through the Regional Interest Zone Embalse de Orellana y Sierra de Pela?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling through the Regional Interest Zone Embalse de Orellana y Sierra de Pela. However, as it is a protected area (SCI and SPA), always adhere to local regulations regarding nature conservation and respect the environment.

faqs.question: Is this racebike tour dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are often allowed in natural areas, this specific route is a long and difficult racebike tour. It's generally not recommended for dogs due to the distance, terrain, and potential for traffic on roads. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, can keep up, and is always on a leash, especially in protected areas.

faqs.question: What gear is recommended for this difficult racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring a well-maintained racebike, appropriate cycling apparel, a helmet, plenty of water and snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Consider tires that can handle potentially rougher sections, especially if you plan to tackle the approach to Puebla de Alcocer Castle.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Orellana La Vieja and Puebla de Alcocer offer options for food and accommodation. As you pass through these towns, you can find cafes, restaurants, and places to stay, which are good spots to refuel or break up your journey.

waypoints.name: Masatrigo Mountain and La Serena Reservoir
